title: ComfyUI Video Models: InfiniteTalk + Wan 2.2 + SCAIL + LTX-2 (Ep06)
description:
In this episode, you’ll learn how to create AI videos inside ComfyUI using the most powerful video models available right now. We cover image-to-video generation, character animation, talking avatars, singing characters with audio, and even cloud-based workflows for those with low VRAM systems.

This tutorial shows you exactly how to install the required models, organize folders correctly, set up workflows, and avoid common mistakes. You’ll also see real examples, performance comparisons, and tips for optimizing generation speed with Sage Attention.

What You’ll Learn
- How to install and organize Wan 2.2 models (high noise & low noise GGUF versions)
- How to generate image-to-video animations in ComfyUI
- How to use Wan Animate for character replacement from a reference video
- How to use Wan Scail for cartoon and stylized animations
- How to create talking AI characters using Infinite Talk
- How to extend Infinite Talk videos up to 34 seconds or more
- How to generate video with audi
transcript: Are you ready for a new episode? Today, we're going to have some fun with video models.  You can even make your characters dance if you want, like this.  Obviously, you can make the character speak, just like I am speaking now using infinite talk,  or we can make it sing using the LTX model, like this.  Before we jump in, quick warning, video models are way heavier than image models.  A 5-second clip at 30 frames per seconds is basically 150 images stitched together,  so it needs more VRAM and it will take longer.  Some setups, like 1.2 with certain GGUF models, can run on 8GB,  but a lot of the workflows in this video, like infinite talk, need much more.  I tested everything on 24GB, and some of it will work on 16GB.  So don't worry if your times are slower, just test and see what your GPU can handle.
